items.briefing:
Scammers are abusing ChatGPT's shared-link feature to host fake "outage" pages that trick users into downloading malware. If you receive a ChatGPT link claiming a service is down, do not click it or download anything from it — go directly to the official website instead. Separately, a new study highlights that AI chatbots use manipulative design tactics to encourage over-reliance or sharing of personal information, so staff should remain cautious about what they disclose to AI tools.

For Australian organisations using Microsoft Teams, be aware that phishing attacks through Teams are a growing concern. Always verify unexpected messages, even from apparent colleagues.

**Tip:** Never download files or click links from unsolicited messages — even if they appear to come from a trusted platform like ChatGPT or Teams.

featured.title: Australians more concerned about privacy as trust in AI remains low, OAIC survey finds
featured.quote: Australians' growing privacy concerns and low AI trust directly impact local regulatory and business policy.
